title: Use a Stove Safely
summary: Turn a stove burner on and off safely and place cookware correctly.
learnerPromise: You will be able to turn a stove burner on and off, place cookware correctly, and follow basic safety habits while cooking.
whyItMatters: Stovetops involve open flame or hot coils that can cause burns or fires if used incorrectly. Safe habits help you cook with confidence and reduce risk.
realLifeUses: Boiling water for pasta on an electric or gas stove
Heating a pan to cook eggs or vegetables
Simmering soup on a back burner
Turning off the stove properly after cooking is done
result: You can light or activate a burner, place cookware safely, and turn everything off correctly.
youWillLearn: Turn a burner on to the correct heat level
Place pots and pans so handles do not stick out over the edge
Keep flammable items away from the heat source
Turn the burner off completely and check that it is off
miniChallenge: Practice turning a stove burner on to medium heat, place a pan on it, then turn it off and double-check that the burner is fully off.
steps: Clear the area around the burner. Remove towels, paper, and other flammable items.
Place your pot or pan in the center of the burner with the handle turned inward.
Turn the burner on to the heat level your recipe or task needs.
Stay nearby while the burner is on. Never leave cooking food unattended.
When done, turn the burner off completely and verify it is off. Move the pan to a safe, heat-resistant surface.
completionCriteria: You turned the burner on and off correctly
The cookware was placed safely with the handle turned inward
You kept the cooking area clear of flammable items
commonProblems: Leaving the handle sticking out where it can be bumped
Turning the burner to the wrong setting (too high or too low)
Walking away and forgetting the stove is on
tips: Always turn the handle inward so no one knocks the pan off the stove.
If a pan catches fire, slide a lid over it and turn off the burner. Do not use water on a grease fire.
Gas stoves: if you smell gas and the burner will not light, turn it off and wait before trying again.
helpBoundary: Ask for help if a fire starts, if you smell gas and cannot find the source, if you get a burn that blisters, or if the stove or burner looks damaged. For fires you cannot control, leave and call emergency services.
